intro: Xantolis maritima, also known as the mangrove xanthisma, is a plant species belonging to the family Sapotaceae, commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions near coastal areas. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Xantolis maritima include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which have been shown to possess antioxidant and free-radical scavenging activities. The plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to alleviate symptoms of fever, inflammation, and skin conditions.
